claim: Mirage is a multi-level superoptimizer for tensor programs using uGraphs, a uniform hierarchical representation at the kernel, thread-block, and thread levels of the GPU compute hierarchy, that jointly searches algebraic transformations, schedule transformations, and new custom kernels with abstraction-based pruning and a probabilistic equivalence verifier (polynomial identity testing over finite fields for the LAX fragment), outperforming prior approaches by 1.1-2.9x

claim: Mirage Persistent Kernel (MPK) is a compiler and runtime that automatically transforms LLM inference into a single megakernel performing all compute and communication in one kernel launch, using an SM-level task graph (tGraph) with cross-operator software pipelining and decentralized SM scheduling, reducing end-to-end inference latency by 1.2x-6.7x
